content.problemStatement:
**Short Answer - 2.5 marks**

Write a program to print all the **leaders** in the array. An element is a leader if it is strictly greater than all the elements to its right side. And the rightmost element is always a leader.


Input Format

The first line contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format
In a single line output all the leaders in the given array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing all the leaders of the given array in the order they appear from left to right, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Scanning from right, leaders are 2, then 5 (greater than 2), then 17 (greater than 5). Reversing gives the order 17 5 2.
All elements are equal, so only the rightmost element qualifies as a leader.
The array is strictly increasing; only the last element is greater than all elements to its right.

solution.approach: A leader is an element that is strictly greater than every element to its right. Scan the array from right to left while keeping the maximum value seen so far. The rightmost element is always a leader. Whenever the current element is greater than the stored maximum, it becomes a new leader and we update the maximum. Store leaders in a temporary list; they will be collected in reverse order, so finally output them reversed to restore left‑to‑right order.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, because each element is visited exactly once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the number of leaders (at most N).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        leaders = []
        max_from_right = arr[-1]
        leaders.append(max_from_right)
        for x in reversed(arr[:-1]):
            if x > max_from_right:
                max_from_right = x
                leaders.append(x)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, reversed(leaders))))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
